question:
These young men, who are often ill-equipped themselves and poorly paid, work daily in dangerous conditions to help climbers achieve their goals.

The underlined relative pronoun 'who' refers to:

I've been trying to persuade people in my community to help an environmental charity by donating just a few coins each week. At first people were not keen, but my persistence has paid off and now a lot of people give me a little money.

Why does the author keep asking people for donations?
choices: Because they want a lot of money.
Because they believe small donations can help.
Because they enjoy talking to people.
Because they want to stop the charity.

question:
As far as I'm concerned, social media is the obvious way to get your message across. All you need to do is to pay for a good Internet connection which most people have anyway these days.

The underlined relative pronoun 'which' refers to:

Last year, I joined a group that wanted to clean up one of the beaches near Aqaba, where I live. I soon realised that volunteering like this helped but didn't solve the problem. The only way is to change tourists' attitudes to litter.

What did the author realise about volunteering?
choices: It completely solved the litter problem.
It helped but didn't solve the problem.
It was not helpful at all.

question: Every day, ________ 10,000 acres of trees are lost.

'Ahmad drinks tea daily.'
She said that ________.

'Sami is a doctor.'
Huda said that ________.

He said that ________.

'Muna didn't study well.'
Ali said that ________.

'She should go out.'
He said that ________.

'How long have you been studying English?'
Ahmad asked us ________.
choices: how long we had been studying English.
for how long we had been studying English.
how long had we been studying English.

question:
'Why didn't you attend the party?'
He asked me ________.
choices: why I hadn't attended the party.
why I didn't attend the party.
the reason why I hadn't attended the party.
why I don't attend the party.

question:
'Leave the room.'
She told me ________.

'Don't eat this cake.'
His mother told him ________.

Laith: 'You should get more exercise, Omar.'
Laith ________.
choices: accused Omar to get more exercise.
encouraged Omar to get more exercise.
advise Omar to get more exercise.

question:
Nour: 'I hear you've graduated, Samia. Congratulations!'
Nour ________.

Hamed: 'I'll always remember you!'
Hamed ________.
choices: promised that he would always remember me.
insisted that he would always remembering me.
promised that he will always remembered me.
encouraged that he always remember me.

question: The conductor ________ if he could see our tickets.
The security guard ________ us not to enter the building.
The police praised Imad ________ saving the man's life.
The musician objected ________ playing only his hits at the concert.
Which phrase expresses a COUNTER opinion?
choices: Without a shadow of a doubt, ...
It's a good idea in principle, but ...

question:
'I'm really sorry I criticised your idea.'
Rola ________.

Read this passage and answer the question:

Yusuf's son, Abbas Yusuf, has continued his father's tradition but in a slightly different way. Every day, when it is getting dark, he calls out to attract the hyenas. Then he picks up some meat and holds it out for them. The animals come up to him and take the meat from his hands.

What does Abbas Yusuf do every day?
choices: He hunts the hyenas to keep people safe.
He calls out to attract hyenas and feeds them meat from his hands.
He puts food behind the city walls for the hyenas.
He trains tourists to feed the hyenas safely.

question:
Read this passage and answer the question:

People explain that nobody in Harar has been attacked by a hyena for 200 years, and some people even say they are useful around the city because they help to eat some of the rubbish from the rubbish tips, helping to discourage flies and other insects from breeding diseases.

According to the passage, how long has nobody in Harar been attacked by a hyena?
